summary: The AdminFilter configuration value is directly interpolated into LDAP search queries without sanitization, allowing LDAP injection attacks that can grant admin privileges to all LDAP users or bypass authorization checks.
expected_security_impact: Attackers with access to LDAP configuration can inject malicious AdminFilter values to escalate privileges for all LDAP users or manipulate the LDAP query logic to bypass authorization.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh demonstrated that the AdminFilter value is directly used in LDAP search queries without sanitization, allowing injection of LDAP filter syntax to manipulate query behavior.
verification_method: The exploit script sets up an OpenLDAP server, configures Gogs with a malicious AdminFilter containing LDAP injection payload, and verifies that all users become admins regardless of their actual group membership.
evidence_produced_by_run: AdminFilter value (objectClass=*) injected into LDAP query
All LDAP users granted admin privileges regardless of group membership
LDAP query logs show the injected filter was executed
limitations_or_blockers: Requires admin access to Gogs configuration to set AdminFilter
Needs a working LDAP server for full demonstration

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Gogs repository from GitHub and checks out the vulnerable version
